db:abstract.db:para: Demonstrates how to use a 2D layer as a 3D texture.

db:article.db:para: This example shows how to use 2D content from Qt Quick and use it as a texture for 3D objects. The 2D item used in this example is a fancy corkboard and it is textured onto a simple double door.

db:section.db:title: The 2D scene
db:section.db:para: The 2D content we use for this example is the CorkBoard scene from Qt Quick's Touch Interaction Example. We put it in a Rectangle and show it on top of our View3D.
We give the 2D object the name object2d, so we can refer to it later.

db:section.db:title: The 3D view
db:section.db:para: To use the 2D content in our 3D scene, we first have to put it into a material. The texture is identified by the sourceItem property, referring back to object2d.

We use the same material for each door, but different meshes. The meshes are designed so that the texture is divided evenly across the doors. In addition to setting the material and the source mesh, we also set the pickable property, so we can click on the doors to open and close them:
Finally, we add some interaction to our 3D scene, using View3D.pick:
Each door will open when clicked and its state is set to "opened".
